summaries.137677: A commenter confirmed reproduction on macOS and web. The root cause was identified as using the selection base offset instead of the selection start offset; a fix landed in #137863 and was confirmed working on master. However, the original reporter demonstrated via video that the fix only addressed horizontal positioning and the bug persists when the TextField widget itself changes size/position as text is typed (e.g., when centered and with many selected lines), which the patch author acknowledged as a narrower remaining case with lower priority.
summaries.84419: Reproduction confirmed on both stable and master on iOS physical and simulator. The root cause was traced in detail: when `setTextInputState:` is called to clear the field, it calls `[self.inputDelegate textWillChange:self]`, which causes `UIDictationController` to flush serialized dictation results via `insertText:`, re-inserting the text after it was cleared. No programmatic way to dismiss dictation or detect its state was found; a workaround using `Future.delayed(Duration(milliseconds: 10))` to call `clear()` a second time was suggested by a commenter. Status: stalled, engine-level issue with no fix.
summaries.93934: A commenter confirmed reproduction on both macOS and Windows desktop (not on web). The issue affects both stable and master channels. No fix or PR was proposed; status is open and stalled.
summaries.115903: Reproduction confirmed on iPadOS stable and master. A commenter identified the root cause: Flutter's keyboard shortcut handling intercepts the Tab key before the platform can handle it for IME candidate cycling. A similar fix for delete/backspace was done in #115900, and the suggestion is to disable the Tab shortcut when the active text input is composing. No PR submitted; status is stalled.
summaries.154055: The issue was confirmed on Windows (dead key generates a character on the first keystroke when using FR input method). A commenter also reproduced a related but distinct problem on Linux and macOS where the second keystroke after a dead key produces `e` instead of the composed character. The issue was confirmed on master 3.25.0-1.0.pre.147 and marked as a framework issue. No PR submitted; status is open.
summaries.98894: Comments clarified that Ctrl+Insert, Shift+Insert, and Shift+Delete (legacy Windows cut/copy/paste shortcuts) do not all work. A commenter initially confirmed most work except Shift+Insert, but the reporter later could not reproduce and suggested a stale build was to blame. The issue was ultimately relabeled as a feature request specifically for those three legacy shortcuts, with the reporter noting they already work on web via the browser. No PR submitted.
summaries.62401: Comments narrowed the root cause to specific Android device keyboards (Samsung, LG) not showing date separator characters when `InputType.TYPE_CLASS_DATETIME` is requested — the same underlying issue exists in native Android APIs. Multiple workarounds discussed: use `initialEntryMode: DatePickerEntryMode.calendarOnly`, expose `keyboardType` parameter (a PR was opened for this), use locale override (e.g., `Locale('en', 'GB')`), or extend `GregorianCalendarDelegate.parseCompactDate`. Status: open, awaiting a proper fix.
summaries.133954: Reproduction confirmed on stable 3.13.2 and master. The error is that `LogicalKeyboardKey.numpadEnter` is not present in the Windows keyCode map used by `KeyEventSimulator`, causing the test assertion to fail. No fix or PR was proposed; status is stalled.
summaries.96199: Comments confirmed the issue is specific to iOS browsers (Safari, Chrome, Edge, Opera, DuckDuckGo) when `TextInputType.number` is used — the up/down navigation arrows are disabled. Android web works correctly. The issue was initially closed as a duplicate of #73835 but reopened as distinct. A 2025 update confirmed the bug persists on stable 3.35.7 and master 3.38.0. Status: open and stalled.
summaries.134265: The root cause was identified: `setCaretRect` is a no-op on web, and the browser positions IME panels based on the element's style (font family, size, transform). When multiple text styles are used, the browser cannot infer the correct caret position. A commenter confirmed this is a fundamental styling limitation — Flutter can express more styles than CSS — and that fixing it requires significant design work. Status: accepted with no clear solution path.
summaries.126900: Reproduction confirmed on Chrome (Android) and Safari (iOS). A commenter noted the issue also occurs with `Element.html`, not just `IFrameElement`. No fix or PR proposed; status is stalled.
summaries.116661: The issue was confirmed on iOS simulator and likely on iOS release mode. The bug manifests when multiple keys are pressed simultaneously just before a line break would be inserted by the `onChanged` formatter — some characters are dropped or the formatter fails to apply. The root cause appears to be a race between rapid key input and the formatter modifying the controller state. No fix proposed; status is stalled.
summaries.139127: Comments confirmed the issue is specific to iOS with Flutter + WebView (not Android, not pure Flutter without WebView, not the default iOS keyboard). A minimal code sample was provided showing that even a Flutter `TextField` alongside a `WebViewWidget` triggers the abnormal Xunfei IME animation. A team member noted this is likely a platform view issue rather than a plugin issue. Status: stalled as of Flutter 3.24.3.
summaries.137255: The root cause was identified in comments: when an `AutofillGroup` contains two inputs with the same autofill hint (e.g., two `newPassword` fields), Flutter assigns the same `id` attribute to both hidden DOM elements, causing the browser warning about non-unique IDs. A commenter proposed decoupling the `id` attribute from the autofill hint or appending an incremental suffix, and noted that duplicate autofill hints are a valid use case (e.g., shipping + billing forms). No PR submitted; status is open.
summaries.85597: Reproduction confirmed on iOS simulator and physical device (not Android). Comments confirm the keyboard disappears when switching focus via `unfocus()`/`requestFocus()` during a `PageView` page transition. An additional commenter reported the same on Android. No fix or PR identified; the issue was left without a concrete root cause or resolution.
summaries.91345: Reproduction confirmed on Linux (stable and master). The issue is that when a modifier key (e.g., ShiftLeft) is held during a hot restart, the engine resets but the Flutter framework still has the key in a pressed state, causing a `KeyUpEvent` assertion failure (`_pressedKeys.containsKey(event.physicalKey)`). The body notes a fix was made in the engine (#28877) but unit testing of `FlView` was not feasible headlessly. Status: the label-cleanup comment suggests ongoing triaging.
summaries.177453: The reporter identified the root cause: `RenderSliverMainAxisGroup` does not report `maxScrollObstructionExtent` in its geometry, so `isPinned` is `false` when calling `getOffsetToReveal`, causing `showOnScreen` to calculate the leading offset as 0 and scroll to the top. A simple fix is to propagate `maxScrollObstructionExtent` from child slivers, but a commenter noted this could cause non-pinned children in a `SliverMainAxisGroup` to not scroll into view. PR #178809 was proposed as a potential fix and is awaiting review from @yiiim.
summaries.161123: Comments established that the issue is environment-dependent: on Ubuntu 20.04 with X11 in fullscreen, the on-screen keyboard stops appearing after the first few uses; on Ubuntu 24.04, the keyboard appears but requires tapping twice. A commenter clarified it works in non-Flutter GTK apps and web browsers in fullscreen. The team noted the behavior differs between X11 (overly responsive) and Wayland (rarely appears), and the issue was labeled for further investigation. Status: open, Linux-specific.
summaries.136014: The crash (`LateInitializationError: Field 'hasPreviousPage' has not been initialized`) was reproduced by a commenter who found it occurs when dragging the Cupertino text selection toolbar horizontally — the drag-end handler fires but the page state fields were never initialized. Multiple users confirmed on Flutter 3.13.x (iOS only). A commenter reproduced it on 3.13 and provided a crashlytics stack trace. Status: open, iOS-specific, no fix proposed.
summaries.171955: Comments confirmed the issue reproduces only on Samsung devices (S10+, Galaxy S20, etc.) and not on Pixel or OnePlus. The native Android implementation has the same behavior, indicating the root cause is Samsung keyboard's autocorrect system racing with the `TextInputFormatter`. The Samsung autocorrect flushes before the formatter's state update propagates, causing the first letter to be dropped from the suggestion. `TextCapitalization.words` is a partial workaround but insufficient for names with particles. Status: labeled for team tracking, no fix actionable from Flutter.
summaries.44183: Comments clarified that on Raspberry Pi without a physical keyboard, no virtual keyboard appears in Chrome in kiosk mode because the OS (Raspbian) does not provide an automatic virtual keyboard for browser apps. The team confirmed Flutter has no plans to implement its own in-framework keyboard. The reporter found success using the `virtual_keyboard` pub package combined with `TextEditingController` updates and focus nodes to show/hide the widget keyboard. A workaround using the `readonly` property to prevent the OS keyboard from appearing on non-Pi platforms was also shared.
summaries.127657: Reproduction confirmed on Android and iOS (macOS/web selection handles not visible there). The root cause is that `CompositedTransformFollower` (used for selection handles) inherits the full transform including rotation from its `CompositedTransformTarget`, so handles appear misplaced on rotated text. A commenter found that wrapping the rotated widget in a `CompositedTransformTarget` with a `LayerLink` directly inside the `Transform.rotate` makes the handles appear correctly, though the reporter considered this a workaround rather than the proper fix. Status: open, no PR.
summaries.162977: The root cause was identified: when the app loses focus (e.g., via Ctrl+L to browser URL bar), the modifier key is not removed from `HardwareKeyboard.instance.logicalKeysPressed`, leaving it stuck in a pressed state. After refocusing the TextField, the `LogicalKeySet` shortcut no longer fires because the system thinks the key is still held. This is the same underlying issue as #99330. Confirmed on stable 3.29.0 and master 3.30.0, on both web and desktop platforms.
summaries.52526: The root cause was identified: `FlexibleSpaceBar` applies a `Transform` widget to scale the app bar content, and `CompositedTransformFollower` (used for the text selection menu) picks up and applies that transform to the overlay, scaling the menu as well. The proposed fix is to use a new `FollowerLayer` that only matches the target's position without inheriting the scale. A commenter suggested counter-scaling the handles as the right approach. Issue confirmed on Android and iOS on latest stable and master. Status: open, no PR.
summaries.166311: Comments confirmed the issue is device-specific to Xiaomi HyperOS (and Nubia) devices with secure keyboard enabled. On these devices, the first tap on an `obscureText: true` TextField gives focus without showing the secure keyboard; a second tap is required. The same issue does not occur on standard Android keyboards. The team labeled it for further investigation as they lack a Xiaomi/Redmi device for reproduction. Status: device-specific, open.
summaries.159977: Comments confirmed this is a regression since Flutter 3.23.0-0.0.pre — before that version (e.g., 3.22.3) the Onboard on-screen keyboard on Linux worked correctly. The issue manifests as the Flutter app freezing for several seconds when the keyboard appears, then suddenly registering all buffered key presses at once. The team confirmed reproduction on stable 3.24.5 through master 3.28.0 but not on stable 3.22.3. One commenter noted a similar but possibly related issue on web mobile. Status: confirmed regression, open.
summaries.122571: Reproduction confirmed on iPad only (not iPhone). The bug is that when a button is pressed while the keyboard is dismissing, the `GestureDetector` gets stuck in pressed state and the `onPressed` handler never fires. The issue persists as of Flutter 3.38.9. No root cause identified in comments; status is stalled.
